hand.purpose: Brand mark, section titles, week entries. The only handwritten voice. Always Caveat 700.

body.purpose: Reading copy, captions, paragraphs. Inter 400/500. Never Georgia, never a condensed display stack.

mono.purpose: Course codes, timestamps, inline data, footer colophon. Special Elite 400. The data voice.

shadows.name: polaroid-lift
shadows.purpose: Subtle paper-on-paper lift for polaroid-framed artifacts only.

shadows.name: tape-lift
shadows.purpose: Tape strip shadow. Used only on .tape and .sticky.

components.name: Highlighter Sweep
components.description: A span of highlighted text, used like a real highlighter behind a single word or short phrase.
components.css: .highlighter { background: #d8ff5c; mix-blend-mode: multiply; padding: 0 6px; display: inline-block; line-height: 1.1; }

components.name: Tape Strip
components.description: A small masking-tape strip used to mount artifacts to the page. Rotated slightly.
components.css: .tape { background: rgba(232, 220, 193, 0.85); padding: 4px 14px; display: inline-block; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.08), inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04); font-family: 'Special Elite', monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; font-size: 0.7rem; color: #4a4a42; }

components.name: Stamp
components.description: A dashed-border date or section stamp. Slightly rotated.
components.css: .stamp { font-family: 'Special Elite', monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.14em; color: #9a958a; border: 1.5px dashed #9a958a; padding: 4px 10px; display: inline-block; }

components.name: Polaroid Frame
components.description: A paper-on-paper mount for screenshots, diagrams, and structured cards.
components.css: .polaroid { background: #fbf8f0; padding: 6px 6px 22px; border: 1px solid rgba(26, 26, 23, 0.18); box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.6) inset, 0 2px 6px rgba(26, 26, 23, 0.10); }

components.name: Margin Note
components.description: A handwritten annotation in the page margin. Caveat 400, ink-soft color.

narrative.overview: A research notebook that is also the product demo. The page looks like a Moleskine opened on a desk: hand-traced diagrams, real data plotted in pen, a fluorescent highlighter pulled over the things that matter. The aesthetic is intentionally made, not templatey — and it practices what Synapse preaches: the data is real, the graph is real, the page itself is a graph. The palette is paper (#f3ead7) with a single highlighter accent (#d8ff5c) reserved for things that matter. A red pen (#c2362a) is the second voice, used only for corrections. Typography is intentionally a hand-drawn display (Caveat) for titles, Inter for body, and a typewriter mono (Special Elite) for data — the page reads like a research notebook, not a SaaS landing page. This system is a complete replacement of the previous warm-paper / green-ink / Georgia / condensed-display system. No anchors are carried forward.
keyCharacteristics: Cream paper surface with a subtle fiber noise, charcoal ink, a fluorescent highlighter as the single accent
Hand-drawn display type (Caveat), Inter for reading, Special Elite for data
Real academic data (course codes, real grades, real deadlines) is the design — not decoration
The hero, the graph section, and the timeline all carry real structured data
Flat surfaces with subtle paper artifacts (polaroid frames, tape strips, dashed borders)

rules.name: The Highlighter Rule
rules.body: The highlighter is the only accent. It must constitute ≤10% of any viewport. Its rarity is the point — when a student sees the highlighter, they know something matters.

rules.name: The Red Pen Rule
rules.body: Red is the second voice. It is used for: corrections, circled to-do items, one or two margin notes, and nothing else. If you find yourself reaching for red three times in a section, you have used it wrong.

rules.name: The Caveat Ceiling
rules.body: Caveat is the only hand-drawn voice and is reserved for titles, the brand, and the week entries. Never use it for body copy, never use it for data, never use it for nav.

dos: Do use the highlighter (#d8ff5c) for one thing per section at most.
Do use the red pen (#c2362a) only for corrections and circled items.
Do mount real academic data: course codes, real deadlines, real weights.
Do rotate small UI elements ±1–3° for the traced-by-hand feel.
Do use polaroid frames, tape strips, and dashed-border stamps as the page furniture.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — there are no autoplay animations anyway.
donts: Don't use the previous system's green ink, warm paper tone, or Georgia body. They're gone.
Don't use glassmorphism, gradient text, or hero-metric stat templates.
Don't use big numbers + small labels.
Don't use a numbered section scaffold (01 / 02 / 03).
Don't add scroll animations, parallax, or entrance transitions.
Don't use the dark network-glow footer.
Don't use perfect geometric bezier curves for arrows — slight jitter is the design.
Don't use rounded-everything. Polaroid corners are 0px, card corners are 2–4px.
Don't carry forward any anchor from the previous design.
